free_text: Pion-induced spallation of copper across the (3,3)
resonance.
Cross section for proton-induced spallation of copper
at 590 MeV.
The targets were electrolytically refined, 3.8-cm diam.
copper disks (Cu purity >99.8%). The thicknesses ranged
from 1.8 g/cm**2 in the earlier runs to 0.4 g/cm**2 in
the later runs at higher beam intensities.
For the proton measurements thinner and smaller foils
were used.
.Intranuclear cascade plus evaporation
calculations.
. Three different Ge(Li) gamma-ray spectrometer
Systems were used to collect the gamma-ray spectra from
the Cu targets.
.The pion fluence in the first run was measured
by placing a 0.32-cm thick*3.8-cm diam. Pilot 'B'
scintillator disk in front contact with the Cu target.
.A 7.6-cm*7.6-cm NaI(Tl) scintillator unit which
detected the annihilation photons.
To determine 18-F disintegration
Rate.
.Used for the pion and the 350-MeV
proton measurements.
.Used for the 200-MeV proton meas'ts.
The Pion fluence was determined by activation of Pilot
'B' (20.4-min C-11) and Al and Si (110-min F-18 and
15-hour NA-24) monitor disks.
The Proton Fluences were Determined from the Amount of
Na-24 Induced in Al Monitor Foils.
Within 15 Minutes After an Irradiation the
Target and Monitor Disks were Removed to a Low
Background, Shielded Environment For Counting the
Induced Radioactivity.
/by Authors/.Corrections were made for photon
absorption in the relatively thick copper disks and
also for the large source diameter (3.8 cm) vs the
point sources of the calibration standards.
- By Compiler - Source of the decay-data used unknown.
The uncertainties listed for the experimental
data arise predominantly from determination of the
photopeak intensities and from estimated uncertainties
in the monitor cross sections. Smaller contributions
arise from corrections for photon absorption in the
target, extended source geometry of the target,
photopeak efficiency of the detector and variations
in beam intensity during the irradiation.
Last checking has been done.
DATE is corrected
SD: In REACTION code (Subent 26) SF5=CUM
was changed on IND. Cosmetic changes.
SD: REACTION code corrected in Subent 49.
